title: Jaborosa sativa
taxonomy: Jaborosa sativa is classified within the plant family Solanaceae, commonly known as the nightshade family. This family comprises various flowering plants, including many species of tomatoes, potatoes, and eggplants. Jaborosa sativa is a genus of flowering plants native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountain range. The Solanaceae family is characterized by its complex reproductive biology and the presence of alkaloids. Jaborosa sativa's classification within this family highlights its evolutionary relationships with other species in the Solanaceae family.

Its roots and leaves are used to prepare infusions and decoctions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, Jaborosa sativa is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is also used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and stomach pain. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
